title: Hohmann Transfer Calculator
description: Build an interactive tool that calculates Hohmann transfer orbits between any two circular orbits. Compute delta-v for each burn, transfer time, and total propellant mass required for a given spacecraft.
whyItMatters: The Hohmann transfer is the most fuel-efficient two-burn transfer between circular orbits and the baseline for all orbit transfer planning. Every satellite deployment, space station rendezvous, and planetary transfer starts with a Hohmann calculation.

steps.title: Implement the maths
steps.description: Code the Hohmann transfer equations: delta-v1, delta-v2, transfer orbit semi-major axis, and transfer time. Handle both raising and lowering orbits.
steps.tips: Transfer time = pi * sqrt(a_transfer^3 / GM). Do not forget — it is half an orbit.

steps.title: Add propellant calculation
steps.description: Using the Tsiolkovsky equation, calculate propellant mass for each burn given spacecraft dry mass and engine Isp.
steps.tips: Apply the equation to each burn separately — the spacecraft is lighter after the first burn.

steps.title: Build the interface
steps.description: Create an interactive calculator (web app or Jupyter notebook) where users input starting orbit, target orbit, spacecraft mass, and engine Isp.
steps.tips: Include preset scenarios: LEO to GEO, LEO to lunar orbit, Earth to Mars transfer.

steps.title: Visualise the transfer
steps.description: Plot the initial orbit, transfer ellipse, and final orbit. Annotate burn points with delta-v values.
steps.tips: Use matplotlib or Plotly for the orbital diagram. Colour-code each orbit segment.

steps.title: Compare with real missions
steps.description: Validate your calculator against published delta-v values for Starlink deployment, GEO satellite insertion, and Apollo lunar transfer.
steps.tips: Real missions add margins of 5-10% for navigation errors and contingency.

deliverables: Comparison table: calculator results vs published mission data
Documentation explaining the maths and assumptions

rubric.description: Delta-v values match analytical solutions exactly
Intuitive input/output with clear units and labels
Accurate orbital diagram with burn locations and transfer ellipse
Calculator validated against at least three real mission profiles
